metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

## Scenario signal

session-start asserts exactly two user-observable bullets: (1) the session appears in `ready` within 1s of agent launch, and (2) its PID is bound within 1s. No tool calls, final state `ready`. Both are timing assertions anchored to *launch*, not to a turn — they are satisfied by the daemon's pre-session (`proc-<PID>`) row, before any transcript turn completes.

## agent = yes

Mistral Vibe (live CLI v2.19.0 at ~/.local/bin/vibe, source under .../uv/tools/mistral-vibe/) launches into an interactive REPL, appends its first line to `~/.vibe/logs/session/session_<ts>_<hash>/messages.jsonl`, and writes a sibling `meta.json` sidecar — then sits idle awaiting input. Six real transcripts on disk confirm the exact envelope the adapter parses (user/assistant-with-tool_calls/tool roles) and the sidecar keys `environment.working_directory`, `config.active_model` (= `mistral-medium-3.5` in every sample), `stats.context_tokens`. Launch + first-line + idle is the whole of session-start, so the agent trivially supports it.

## daemon = full

The vibe adapter (built this session, commit e943e261) registers `Process.Match: agent.CommandPattern{Regex: processCmdRegex}` with `PIDForSession: DiscoverPID`. CommandPattern is a first-class matcher kind (handled in `diagnostics_service.go:325`), and the processlifecycle scanner mints a `proc-<PID>` pre-session row in `ready` for any matched process — the same mechanism that satisfies claudecode's session-start via its `ExactName` matcher (see `session_detector_activity.go:205`, `pid_manager.go:684-693`). Vibe's process is a Python console-script, so an `ExactName` on "vibe" would never fire; the CommandPattern anchors on the uv tool-venv interpreter path / a `/vibe ` token. `DiscoverPID` binds the PID by cwd (from the sidecar) + cmdline match, at the same scan tick that mints the pre-session row. Bullet (1) ready-within-1s and bullet (2) PID-within-1s are therefore both covered by the pre-session row, independent of when `messages.jsonl` materializes — exactly the claudecode session-start shape. None of the daemon's known vibe gaps (no per-turn cost; no context-WINDOW resolution because mistral models are absent from core/pkg/capacity; per-event timestamps backfilled to scan time; no Control/backchannel; no subagent linking) touch either session-start bullet — they are caveats/follow-ups, not spec-required observables here.

## driver = gap:wait_turn

The interactive driver `replaydata/agents/mistral-vibe/driver-interactive.sh` is freshly scaffolded. Only `send`/`slash`/`sleep` step arms are real. The recipe (mirroring claudecode's session-start: send a nudge → wait_turn → sleep) needs `wait_turn`, but the driver's SEAM-2 `wait_turn()` (line 169) dispatches to `not_implemented` (exits 3). Two further unported seams block recording of ANY vibe cell today and must be ported alongside: SEAM 3 — `launch_repl` (line 158) never resolves/stores the transcript path into `SES_TRANSCRIPT[$ACTIVE]`, so `emit_session_contract` (line 210) records no session for `record` to verify; and SEAM 1 — the launch command (line 155) execs `"mistral-vibe"`, which is NOT on PATH (only `vibe` is), so the REPL never starts. `record` ports SEAM 1 (fix the binary to `vibe`), SEAM 2 (`wait_turn` turn-done poll on messages.jsonl), and SEAM 3 (transcript-path capture) before it can drive this cell. This is tooling work, not an observability limit — the cell keeps its recipe + spec.
caveats: Provisional on live drive: daemon=full is derived from reading the adapter + shared scanner; the pre-session row minting for a CommandPattern matcher is the same code path as claudecode's ExactName, but a vibe recording has not yet confirmed the timing budget end-to-end. record promotes this from provisional to settled.

The model name mistral-medium-3.5 displays, but its context WINDOW is unresolved — mistral models are absent from core/pkg/capacity, so the utilization bar is a follow-up. Does not affect session-start (no context bar asserted).
The daemon does not emit per-turn cost for vibe (the sidecar retains only last-turn tokens, not backfillable), so the spec asserts tokens_nonzero + model but NOT cost.
messages.jsonl carries no per-event timestamp; backfill uses scan time. Irrelevant to session-start's launch-anchored bullets.

sources.note: Process.Match = CommandPattern{Regex: processCmdRegex}, PIDForSession = DiscoverPID, Source = FilesUnderRoot on ~/.vibe/logs/session with SessionIDFromPath = the session_<...> dir; no Control declared (read-only).
DiscoverPID binds the PID by cwd (sidecar fallback) + cmdline match via processlifecycle.DiscoverPIDByCWDAndCmdLine — feeds the pid_discovered event.
Lines 684-693: proc-<pid> pre-sessions are minted by processlifecycle/scanner.go and encode their PID in the session id — the ready-within-1s + PID-within-1s mechanism, matcher-kind agnostic.
Line 169: wait_turn() -> not_implemented (stub). Line 158: SEAM 3 transcript-path capture unported (: no-op). Line 155: launch execs "mistral-vibe", not on PATH (only vibe is).

Live sidecar: config.active_model=mistral-medium-3.5, environment.working_directory set, stats.context_tokens=136662, session_cost present, parent_session_id=null (vibe subagents are in-process, not file-based).

preconditions: vibe CLI on PATH (~/.local/bin/vibe, v2.19.0) and authenticated (Mistral API key)
irrlichd is recording (run-cell.sh isolated mode, or --attach against a --record daemon)
driver seams ported by record: SEAM 1 launch binary vibe (not mistral-vibe), SEAM 2 wait_turn poll, SEAM 3 transcript-path capture
setup: Create a fresh empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ so the session's working_directory is unique
Vibe mints its own session_<ts>_<hash> id on launch; the daemon session_id is that directory name

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl contains a presession_created event for proc-<PID> within 1000 ms of the tmux new-session timestamp
events.jsonl contains a state_transition to ready for proc-<PID> within 100 ms of presession_created
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event within 1000 ms
events.jsonl contains a transcript_new event for the session_<ts>_<hash> session once messages.jsonl materializes
meta.json sidecar supplies model mistral-medium-3.5 and a non-zero context_tokens on turn_done
